_comment: Curated semantics for the Pulse component index. HAND-OWNED. The structural facts (param names, types, whether they have defaults) are PARSED FROM SOURCE by tools/PulseIndex.java and must NOT be duplicated here — this file only carries what source cannot express: role, perfTier, since, and agent-adaptation guidance. Every public component in pulse-ui/src/main/java/design/pulse/ui/components/ must have an entry here or the drift-check fails.

agentMeta.extend: Add new parameters only, each with a backward-compatible default, so existing call sites compile and render pixel-identically. Never change or remove an existing parameter's default.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

neverDoExtra: Do not use for text that changes every frame — the roll animates on the DATA motion (~600ms) and is meant for occasional, measured updates.
'conditional' perfTier: this tile is static UNLESS you pass animatedValue (rolls via TickerNumber) or sparkline. Prefer the static form unless a live metric genuinely needs motion.
The dense metric layout is opt-in via dense=true; leaving it false must stay pixel-identical to the standard tile.
Do NOT assume Sparkline self-animates — it is a pure Canvas draw with no internal animation. Any motion comes from the caller changing the values list.
asBars=true scales bars to the series max from a zero baseline; asBars=false draws a line (min-max normalized by default). Pass a non-null strokeWidth for the filled line-chart mode.

agentMeta.extend: Add new parameters only, each defaulted. Use one hero PulseButton per screen; secondary actions take tonal=true (channel dim fill, channel text) or a plain M3 button.
neverDoExtra: Switch the button's voice with the gradient param (e.g. Pulse.structure.energyGradient), not by hard-coding a Brush of literal colors.
The center is a content slot — put a DataText readout in it rather than overlaying your own absolutely-positioned text.

agentMeta.extend: This is a Modifier extension, not a composable. Feed it the same MutableInteractionSource as the click handler so the spring tracks real presses.
neverDoExtra: Do not stack multiple pressScale modifiers on one element.

agentMeta.extend: Add defaulted params only. Pass a custom colors list to tint a burst to one data domain; otherwise it uses the four brand hues (PulseConfettiColors).
neverDoExtra: 'conditional' perfTier: renders nothing until play=true, then emits one 200ms burst. Do not leave play=true (it re-emits on recomposition) — drive it from a one-shot event and reset it.
Do not fire on routine actions. Confetti is a signal reserved for genuine milestones (a PR, a goal hit, onboarding complete), not every save.

agentMeta.extend: Wrap the thing being celebrated (a badge, a checkmark) as content. Pass a channel Color to tint the glow to a data domain; it defaults to the app's lead accent (Pulse.accent).
neverDoExtra: This self-animates on an infinite transition — it is the quiet celebration. Use it for a sustained badge glow, not a one-shot burst (that is ConfettiHost).
The active dot animates its width on page change; drive it from the pager's current page, don't animate it yourself.

agentMeta.extend: A standard intro page: optional illustration slot + title + body, centered. Put a glyph, image, or CelebrationPulse in the illustration slot; keep the copy app-side.
The intro-pager shell (pager + PulsePageIndicator + Skip + Next/Done). Supply each page via the page slot (usually an OnboardingPage) and put finish behavior (persist 'seen', navigate) in onFinish.
neverDoExtra: Do not put onboarding state/persistence into Pulse — the scaffold is presentation only; the 'has seen onboarding' flag and page content live app-side.

agentMeta.extend: The pick-one/pick-many card. Pass a channel to bind selection to a data domain; override the trailing slot for a custom affordance (radio/checkbox). Selected fills with the channel dim tone.
neverDoExtra: Do not hard-code the selected fill — it comes from the channel's dim tone (Pulse.accent.dim or a passed channelDim).

agentMeta.extend: A 2-4 way exclusive choice (the Dark/Light/System appearance switch is built from it). String labels + selectedIndex + onSelect(index); map your enum to labels app-side. Pass a channel to bind the selection to a data domain.
neverDoExtra: Keep it to a small number of segments — for many options use a list of PulseSelectableCard instead.
The selected fill is the channel dim tone; do not hard-code it.

agentMeta.extend: Pass intensities as one value per day in chronological order; it lays them out in columns of 7. Cell alpha scales over max (series max when null). Bind meaning with a channel.
neverDoExtra: This is a pure Canvas draw with no self-animation. Do not push date/label semantics in — it only knows a chronological list of magnitudes.

agentMeta.extend: Vertical bars for a small set of periods, with x labels. Bars grow in on the DATA motion the first time they compose (and re-grow when values change). The last bar is emphasized; pass emphasizeLast=false to level them.
neverDoExtra: For a tiny inline series with no labels use Sparkline(asBars=true) instead — this is the full labelled version.

agentMeta.extend: A trend line with area fill that wipes in left-to-right on the DATA motion. Min-max normalized by default (fills the height); pass normalizeMinMax=false for a zero baseline.
neverDoExtra: For a tiny inline trend use Sparkline (line mode) instead — this is the full-height version with the draw-in reveal.
